steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes the mutualism rule and its exception: when aphid colonies are too small to produce significant honeydew, ants prey on aphids instead of protecting them. Aphids require stable woody surfaces to thrive, but both of Kwon's habitats are predominantly grassland — unsuitable for large aphid colonies. The blank must describe what Kwon observed, and the grassland setting must ‘help explain’ that observation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — different ant and aphid species on the mainland compared to those on the island.: The passage says the two habitats ‘differed in some important respects’ but focuses on abundance, not species identity. Grassland does not specifically explain different species composition. ✗
  • Choice B — a positive correlation between aphid diversity and abundance on both the mainland and the island.: The passage discusses mutualism and predation based on colony size, not diversity. Grassland does not specifically predict a positive diversity-abundance link. ✗
  • Choice C — a negative correlation between ant and aphid abundance on both the mainland and the island.: Grassland prevents large, productive aphid colonies, so ants prey on aphids rather than protecting them. More ants would mean fewer aphids — a negative correlation. Grassland in both habitats explains why both show this predatory dynamic. ✓
  • Choice D — diminished ant and aphid abundance on the mainland compared to that on the island.: This compares the two habitats against each other. The passage says grassland characterizes both habitats and asks what it ‘could help explain’ about observations in both — not a between-site difference. ✗
That is the correct answer.
